title: invitations — HTTP middleware
description: Middleware this module contributes. Registered by `InvitationsServiceProvider::boot()` via `Router::aliasMiddleware()`. Only `throttle.invitations` is invitation-specific; the module also relies on framework + workspaces middleware composed on its route groups.

middleware.description: Per-inviter + per-workspace rate limits. Enforces four counters simultaneously: (1) per (inviter_user_id, day) — default 50; (2) per (inviter_service_account_id, day) — default 500; (3) per (workspace_id, day) — default 250; (4) per (invitee_email, week) — default 5 (anti-harassment). Each limit is configurable via `invitations.throttle.*` settings; a 429 response includes `Retry-After` and the specific limit that tripped in the `X-RateLimit-Trigger` header.
sideEffects: Bumps a Redis-backed counter per counter dimension using Laravel's `RateLimiter` facade with keys namespaced by `invitations:throttle:{dimension}:{id}`.
Emits `InvitationRateLimitExceeded` (via metrics.json counter — not a domain event) so ops can alert on abuse patterns.

Writes an `InvitationEvent { event: 'preflight_failed', error_code: 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }` row if the caller was authenticated (audit trail; anon requests skipped to avoid a write-amplification vector).

middleware.description: Framework throttle applied to the public token-flow endpoints. Rate: 60/min per IP for GET (preview), 10/min per IP for POST (accept/decline). Distinct from `throttle.invitations` which gates admin-side sends.

notes: Webhook signature verification lives inside the `MailTransportWebhookController`, not middleware — each transport (SendGrid / SES / Postmark / Mailgun) has a different signing scheme and must be handled by transport-specific validators.
Bounce webhooks are IP-allow-listed at the load-balancer layer as defence-in-depth, but the application still verifies signatures — do not rely on the network layer alone.
